reasoning: Under Article 21 of the German Basic Law, only the Federal Constitutional Court (BVerfG) has the authority to ban a political party. Even if a petition were successfully filed by the Bundestag, Bundesrat, or Federal Government in early 2025, banning a party requires an extensive judicial fact-finding process and trials. Historically, the two successful bans in Germany (SRP in 1952, KPD in 1956) took over a year and five years respectively, while recent attempts to ban the NPD took several years each (2001–2003, and 2013–2017). A decision by July 1, 2025 (less than 6 months from the January 2025 debates) is procedurally impossible. Therefore, the probability of an official ban on the AfD before July 1, 2025, is virtually zero.
